human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for food-and-beverage cold-chain specificity, source quality, handoff discipline, excursion and release-boundary protection, commercialization completeness, and explicit specification-based runtime positioning.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cold-chain dependency intake, handoff preparation, logger and traceability evidence review, excursion packaging, blocker escalation, and closure confirmation inside food-and-beverage manufacturing.

Authority sources cover FDA sanitary transportation, FDA traceability and preventive-controls baselines, FDA refrigerated RTE operating guidance, GS1 event and traceability standards, and SQF storage and distribution controls.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete prerequisites, duplicate ownership, traceability conflicts, quality holds, supplier substitution pressure, low-confidence handling, conflicting policy handling, and closure confirmation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-and-beverage-manufacturing/cold-chain-coordination-specialist/ so the lane stays distinct from adjacent quality, warehouse, logistics, production planning, procurement, inventory, and supplier-quality ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final release, excursion disposition, supplier approval, transport execution, and unsupported hold overrides.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated food-and-beverage manufacturing cold-chain coordination specialist that supports evidence-backed handoffs, blocker management, traceability conflict handling, and excursion-safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately approved retained implementation is promoted.
